mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% triggers a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 900,000 units triggers a demand shock that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production below 100 triggers a supply shock that reduces corporate profitability and boosts equity risk premia, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity prices and boosts risk premia, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decrease in payroll employment below 135,000 triggers a labor market downturn that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in labor market downturn periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real GDP above $15,000 billion triggers a demand shock that boosts economic growth and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand shock periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production below 100 triggers a supply shock that reduces corporate profitability and boosts equity risk premia, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
